differential_diagnoses: Severe insulin resistance syndrome (Type B insulin resistance)
Syndrome of insulin resistance due to anti-insulin receptor antibodies
Lipodystrophy associated with autoimmune connective tissue disease
next_steps: Monitor insulin levels and C-peptide during periods of hyperglycemia to rule out factitious disorder
Consider discontinuation of high-dose exogenous insulin if insulin resistance is confirmed to be mediated by autoantibodies
Screen for autoimmune comorbidities (e.g., lupus, Sjogren's syndrome)
Re-evaluate abdominal pain etiology in the context of extreme insulin resistance and lipodystrophy (consider mesenteric ischemia or rare vascular manifestations)
Consult endocrinology, immunology, and rheumatology for specialized management
